statement: The Gallup Þjóðarpúls survey (19 February – 4 March 2026, n≈810) found that a majority of Icelandic respondents supported holding a national referendum on resuming EU accession negotiations. According to the published results, roughly 60% of respondents supported the referendum, approximately 30% opposed it, and approximately 10% were undecided or did not respond. The survey found sharp party-based divisions: voters of Viðreisn (Reform Party) and Samfylkingin (Social Democrats) were the most strongly supportive, while voters of Miðflokkurinn (Centre Party) and Sjálfstæðisflokkurinn (Independence Party) were the most strongly opposed. The survey measured support for holding a referendum on resuming negotiations — not support for EU membership itself, which typically polls lower. This distinction is important: a voter may support giving people the chance to vote on the question while personally opposing EU membership.
caveats: Pre-referendum polling can diverge significantly from actual voting outcomes. Iceland's small population means that campaign dynamics, events, and high-profile endorsements can shift opinion rapidly. The distinction between 'support for a referendum' and 'support for EU membership' is crucial — historically, support for holding a vote runs higher than support for the substantive position. Poll results are also sensitive to question wording and ordering effects.

related_entries.statement: The Gallup Þjóðarpúls (National Pulse) EU referendum survey conducted from 19 February to 4 March 2026 used an online self-completion methodology. The survey was distributed to a sample of 1,898 individuals from the Gallup online panel, drawn from the national register (Þjóðskrá Íslands). A total of approximately 810 respondents completed the survey, yielding a response rate of 42.7%. Gallup applies post-stratification weighting by age, gender, region, and education to ensure the sample is representative of the Icelandic population aged 18 and over. The margin of error for a completed sample of approximately 800 at the 95% confidence level is approximately ±3.5 percentage points for national-level estimates. For subgroup analyses (e.g., by party affiliation), the margins of error are larger due to smaller effective sample sizes. The survey was published in collaboration with Vísir media.

EU support varies dramatically by party affiliation. Among Viðreisn voters, ~90% support EU membership; among Samfylkingin voters, ~75%. Píratar voters show ~55% support, and Left-Green voters ~45%. On the opposing side, Sjálfstæðisflokkur voters show ~25% support, Framsóknarflokkur voters ~20%, Miðflokkur voters ~10%, and Flokkur fólksins voters ~10%. These figures (from late 2025 polling) suggest that the referendum will cut across party lines, with significant minorities within each party diverging from the official party position. The ~25% of Independence Party voters who support membership is particularly significant given that party's large vote share (~22% in recent elections).

The March 2026 Gallup/Vísir survey on the EU referendum showed sharp party-based divisions: Viðreisn voters are most supportive of continuing negotiations, followed by Samfylkingin voters. Miðflokkurinn and Sjálfstæðisflokkurinn voters are most opposed. Framsóknarflokkurinn voters are roughly evenly split, with approximately half opposed. Among those who support the current government, 83% back continuing EU negotiations. The party divide mirrors the coalition/opposition split: the two explicitly pro-EU coalition parties (Samfylkingin, Viðreisn) have strongly pro-negotiation voter bases, while the opposition parties that have historically opposed EU membership retain EU-sceptic electorates.

citations.report_title: Snorri segir húsnæði verða jafndýrt þótt vextir lækki

claims.claim_text: Þingsályktunartillaga um þjóðaratkvæðagreiðslu um aðildarviðræður Íslands að ESB var tekin til umræðu á Alþingi þann 10. mars 2026.

citations.report_title: Þjóðin á báðum áttum um viðræður: 52% gegn 48%

claims.claim_text: Meirihluti landsmanna, eða 57%, er hlynntur þjóðaratkvæðagreiðslu um framhald aðildarviðræðna við Evrópusambandið.
Ríflega 30% landsmanna eru andvíg þjóðaratkvæðagreiðslu um framhald aðildarviðræðna.
Kjósendur Viðreisnar og Samfýlkingarinnar eru helst hlynntir þjóðaratkvæðagreiðslu um framhald aðildarviðræðna.
Kjósendur Flokks fólksins eru einnig hlynntir þjóðaratkvæðagreiðslu um framhald aðildarviðræðna.
Kjósendur Viðreisnar og Samfýlkingarinnar telja helst að þau myndu kjósa með framhaldi aðildarviðræðna fremur en kjósendur stjórnarandstöðuflokkanna.
Meirihluti landsmanna er hlynntur þjóðaratkvæðagreiðslu um framhald aðildarviðræðna við ESB samkvæmt Þjóðarpúlsi Gallup.
Tæpur þriðjungur landsmanna er andvígur þjóðaratkvæðagreiðslunni.
Kjósendur Viðreisnar eru hvað hlynntastir þjóðaratkvæðagreiðslunni.
